description: V6: V5 + 6 historically documented gaps filled (naval blockade, secret treaties, Asquith-Lloyd George transition, post-Armistice blockade, moral-rhetoric vs realist-practice tension, mandate system contradictions). Rebased on committed V5 JSON. 13 issue augmentations + Romania following correction (pre-1916 neutral and post-1917 Little Entente alignment). NOTE: The per-round per-country expected_primary/expected_secondary action labels were generated programmatically by generate_history_v2.py using a default lookup table (e.g., AUH defaults to Military/Diplomatic for all rounds). Unlike the following data (hand-reviewed across V3-V6), these action labels have NOT been independently validated against the historical record and should not be used as an evaluation target without audit.
following_definition: 追随!=同盟: 追随是对某国在特定议题上的领导偏好, 不等同于战略同盟关系。盟友可能在经济议题上追随敌对阵营的领导者, 这是正常的外交现象。每轮的追随基于该轮最突出议题上的政策协调对象。

key_issue_specific_cases.France(4): Ally=Russia(2), follows UK(3) on naval/colonial issues. July Crisis(R7) temporarily follows Russia on security.
key_issue_specific_cases.Italy(6): Triple Alliance member with Germany(1), follows UK(3) on colonial/naval issues - classic issue-based defection.
key_issue_specific_cases.Netherlands(13): Economic ties to Germany(1), follows UK(3) on sea power/trade issues.
key_issue_specific_cases.Switzerland(17): German-speaking economic ties to Germany(1), follows UK(3) on security balance issues.

1.dominant_issue: London Conference - Balkan territorial settlement, UK leads diplomacy

2.dominant_issue: Second Balkan War - Bulgaria vs Serbia/Greece, Russia backs Slavic alliance

3.dominant_issue: Treaty of Bucharest - Balkan territorial resettlement, A-H vs Russia for influence

4.dominant_issue: Liman von Sanders crisis - German military mission to Ottoman, Russia-Germany tension

5.dominant_issue: Anglo-German naval race peaks - UK dominates maritime order, Germany accelerates shipbuilding

6.dominant_issue: Colonial competition & alliance consolidation - Franco-Russian alliance strengthens

7.dominant_issue: July Crisis - diplomatic crisis management, states choose between war and peace

8.dominant_issue: WWI outbreak - mobilization and initial battles, alliance camps coordinate militarily

9.dominant_issue: British naval blockade of Germany escalates & Ottoman Empire enters war -- UK declares North Sea 'military area' (Nov 1914), expands contraband to foodstuffs via Orders in Council, distant blockade departs from Declaration of London (1909); Ottoman attacks Russia, Entente opens Dardanelles front

10.dominant_issue: Italy joins Entente (Treaty of London) & British blockade tightens -- March 1915 Reprisal Order empowers seizure of 'commodities of any kind' bound for Germany; US protests blockade as 'paper blockade' violating Declaration of Paris (1856); Italy switches sides, follows UK/French war strategy against A-H

11.dominant_issue: Eastern Front crisis - Russian Great Retreat, Serbia overrun

12.dominant_issue: Serbia falls & Bulgaria joins Central Powers, blockade humanitarian impact emerges -- Entente forces land at Salonika; German food supply reduced ~25%, 'Turnip Winter' begins; Commission for Relief in Belgium (Hoover) negotiates blockade exceptions for occupied Belgium

13.dominant_issue: Battle of Verdun begins - Western Front war of attrition

14.dominant_issue: Battle of Jutland & Portugal enters war - UK maintains naval supremacy

15.dominant_issue: Somme & Brusilov Offensive - simultaneous major offensives, Romania joins Entente

16.dominant_issue: Romania overrun, Franz Joseph dies, & Asquith government falls -- Central Powers occupy Bucharest; UK leadership transition: Lloyd George replaces Asquith (Dec 1916), instituting small War Cabinet and total mobilization; blockade causing acute German civilian hardship (food riots in 30+ German cities by mid-1916)

17.dominant_issue: Russian Revolution (Feb) & US enters war - international landscape transforms

18.dominant_issue: French army mutinies, Greece joins Entente, & blockade reaches peak severity -- German civilian malnutrition peaks ('Turnip Winter' 1916-17), ~37% of Leipzig households borderline caloric; Entente internal coordination strengthens, Balkan front reopens

19.dominant_issue: Russian October Revolution, Caporetto, & Bolsheviks expose secret Allied treaties -- Trotsky publishes Sykes-Picot (1916), Treaty of London (1915), Hussein-McMahon correspondence; Arab leaders outraged by contradictory promises, Zionist/British relations strained; Italy needs UK/France support after Caporetto defeat

20.dominant_issue: Passchendaele & Cambrai - new technology warfare (tanks, aircraft)

21.dominant_issue: Brest-Litovsk Treaty & German Spring Offensive preparations - Russia exits, Germany masses west

22.dominant_issue: German Spring Offensive & Allied unified command (Foch) - A-H/Ottoman/Bulgaria wavering

23.dominant_issue: Hundred Days Offensive & Central Powers collapse - Bulgaria & Ottoman surrender

24.dominant_issue: Armistice & continued Allied blockade -- WWI fighting ends, German and Austro-Hungarian empires dissolve, UK dominates postwar world order; BUT naval blockade maintained (Nov 1918-Jul 1919) to coerce German acceptance of Versailles terms; ~100,000 additional German civilian deaths from starvation during post-Armistice period; Quaker/American Relief Administration begins child feeding programs (Quakerspeisung) serving 5M+ children

25.dominant_issue: Paris Peace Conference -- UK/France dominate postwar settlement, Wilson's Fourteen Points vs. secret treaty legacy; 'open covenants openly arrived at' clashes with Sykes-Picot/Balfour; Arab delegation (Faisal) demands self-determination, Lloyd George privately negotiates Mosul & Palestine with Clemenceau (Dec 1918 Agreement); League Covenant drafted

26.dominant_issue: Treaty of Versailles signed -- Germany accepts war guilt, reparations (inflated by UK war pensions clause), territorial losses, military restrictions; Lloyd George's Fontainebleau Memorandum (Mar 1919) warned against 'Carthaginian peace' yet UK gained ~1M sq miles of new territory; blockade formally ends Jul 1919 after German signature; self-determination proclaimed while British Empire reaches territorial zenith

27.dominant_issue: Treaty of Saint-Germain - Austria-Hungary formally dissolved, Central Europe redrawn

28.dominant_issue: Treaty of Neuilly & Greco-Turkish War begins -- Greece lands at Smyrna (backed by Lloyd George), Ottoman Empire partitioned per secret Sykes-Picot framework (now publicly implemented as mandates); Arab Kingdom promised to Sharif Hussein reduced to British/French spheres; wartime promises (Hussein-McMahon, Balfour Declaration, Sykes-Picot) visibly contradict each other in postwar settlement

29.dominant_issue: League of Nations founded & mandate system established -- UK/France lead new collective security order, US Senate rejects membership; 'self-determination' removed from Covenant text by British-led negotiators; Class A/B/C mandate hierarchy subordinates Arab/Middle Eastern/African populations to colonial tutelage; Jan Smuts distinguishes between 'civilized' European peoples and 'barbarians' -- racial hierarchy embedded in mandate system; British Empire gains Palestine, Iraq mandates despite wartime Arab independence promises

30.dominant_issue: Treaty of Trianon & Little Entente - Central European order established, France leads Little Entente

31.dominant_issue: Polish-Soviet War - Red Army threatens Central Europe, West aids Poland

32.dominant_issue: Greco-Turkish War intensifies & Treaty of Sevres - Ottoman Empire partitioned

33.dominant_issue: Reparations negotiations -- London Conference sets German reparations total (132B gold marks); France demands harsh enforcement; UK war pensions inclusion inflated bill; German 'war guilt' clause (Article 231) fuels nationalist resentment; Keynes publishes 'Economic Consequences of the Peace' criticizing Versailles; blockade humanitarian legacy feeds German revisionism

34.dominant_issue: Anglo-Soviet Trade Agreement - UK leads economic engagement with Soviet Russia

35.dominant_issue: Treaty of Rapallo -- Germany-Soviet secret cooperation, Germany breaks Versailles isolation, Weimar diplomacy challenges postwar order; Middle East mandates consolidating under British/French control despite Arab Revolt promises; Zionist immigration to Palestine increasing under British mandate, early Arab-Zionist tensions emerge; Balfour Declaration contradictions become visible

36.dominant_issue: Washington Naval Treaty - naval disarmament (5-power treaty)

37.dominant_issue: Genoa Conference - European economic reconstruction, Soviet Russia invited

38.dominant_issue: Treaty of Rapallo - Germany-Soviet secret cooperation, Germany breaks Versailles isolation

39.dominant_issue: Chanak Crisis & Mussolini's March on Rome - UK faces Turkey alone, Italian fascist regime

40.dominant_issue: Mussolini in power & Lausanne Conference - Italy turns to independent fascist foreign policy

41.dominant_issue: Ruhr Crisis - France occupies Ruhr unilaterally, UK opposes French hardline policy

42.dominant_issue: German hyperinflation - economic crisis threatens European stability

43.dominant_issue: Corfu Incident - Italy bombards Greece, League of Nations crisis

44.dominant_issue: Stresemann & Dawes Plan - German economic stabilization, international coordination

45.dominant_issue: Dawes Plan adopted - German reparations restructured, UK/US lead reconstruction

46.dominant_issue: Geneva Protocol - collective security mechanism strengthened

47.dominant_issue: London Conference - final reparations settlement, French evacuate Ruhr

48.dominant_issue: Locarno preparations - Franco-German rapprochement, UK guarantees Western borders

49.dominant_issue: Locarno negotiations - Germany accepts Western borders, European reconciliation

50.dominant_issue: Locarno Treaties signed - European collective security system established

UKG.pre_switch: H.H. Asquith (Liberal, deliberative Cabinet chairman, 'wait and see')
UKG.post_switch: David Lloyd George (Liberal, dynamic War Cabinet executive, 'Easterner' strategist)
